15. ITEM NO. | SCHEDULE OF SUPPLIES/SERVICES | QUANTITY | UNIT | UNIT PRICE | AMOUNT
    0001       Basic Research: Multi-Agent     1          Lot    $749,825.00   $749,825.00
               Autonomous Navigation in
               GPS-Denied Environments

    TOTAL ESTIMATED COST: $749,825.00

16. TOTAL AMOUNT OF CONTRACT: $749,825.00

Security Classification: Unclassified
Distribution Statement A: Approved for public release; distribution unlimited.

---

SECTION B - SUPPLIES OR SERVICES AND PRICES/COSTS

Contract Type: Cost Reimbursement (No Fee)
Award Number: W911NF-24-1-0178

Principal Investigator: Dr. Elena M. Kowalski, Ph.D.
   Department of Computer Science
   University of Montana
   Social Sciences Building, Room 402
   Missoula, MT 59812
   Phone: (406) 555-4129
   Email: elena.kowalski@umontana.edu

Government Program Manager: Dr. James T. Henderson
   Army Research Office
   Mathematical and Information Sciences Division
   Research Triangle Park, NC 27709
   Phone: (919) 555-3081

---

SECTION C - STATEMENT OF WORK

Title: Multi-Agent Autonomous Navigation in GPS-Denied Environments

The contractor shall conduct basic research on decentralized multi-agent navigation
algorithms for teams of unmanned ground vehicles (UGVs) operating in GPS-denied
environments. The research focuses on three technical objectives: (1) development of
visual-inertial odometry algorithms robust to dynamic lighting conditions, (2) design
of consensus-based formation control protocols with provable convergence guarantees,
and (3) field demonstration of a 4-robot team navigating an urban-representative
environment without GPS or prior maps.

---

SECTION D - PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE

Base Period: 01 September 2024 through 31 August 2027 (36 months)
Period of Performance Start: 01 September 2024
Period of Performance End: 31 August 2027

---

SECTION J - COST/PRICE SUMMARY

COST ELEMENT SUMMARY (Full Period of Performance)

1.  Direct Labor                          $318,500
    a. PI (Dr. Kowalski) - 2 SM/yr         $72,000
    b. Postdoctoral Researcher - 12 mo/yr  $168,000
    c. Graduate Students (2) - hourly       $78,500

2.  Fringe Benefits                        $89,600
    a. PI (32.5%)                           $23,400
    b. Postdoc (28%)                        $47,040
    c. Graduate (24.5%)                     $19,233

3.  Equipment                             $45,000
    a. UGV platforms (4 units)              $32,000
    b. LIDAR sensors (4 units)              $13,000

4.  Travel                                $18,200
    a. Conference travel (ICRA, IROS)       $12,000
    b. ARO PI meeting                        $3,200
    c. Field test site travel                $3,000

5.  Materials and Supplies                 $28,500
    a. Computing hardware (GPUs)            $14,000
    b. Sensor components and cables          $8,500
    c. Batteries and consumables             $6,000

6.  Other Direct Costs                     $22,000
    a. Publication fees                      $5,000
    b. Cloud computing services             $12,000
    c. Equipment maintenance                 $5,000

7.  Subcontracts                           $0

8.  TOTAL DIRECT COSTS                    $521,800

9.  Overhead (On-Campus Rate: 43.7% MTDC) $228,025
    Base: $521,800

10. G&A Rate:                              N/A
    (University uses overhead rate only)

11. Fee/Profit:                            $0
    (Cost reimbursement, no fee)

12. TOTAL ESTIMATED COST:                 $749,825

SECTION B - SUPPLIES OR SERVICES AND PRICES/COSTS

Contract Type: Cost-Plus-Fixed-Fee (CPFF)
Award Number: N00014-25-C-1042

Principal Investigator: Dr. Rachel S. Whitmore, Ph.D.
   Chief Scientist, Undersea Systems Division
   Meridian Defense Systems, Inc.
   Phone: (703) 555-8847
   Email: rwhitmore@meridiandefense.com
   Security Clearance: SECRET

Government Program Manager: Mr. Kevin D. Park
   Office of Naval Research
   Code 321 - Ocean Acoustics
   Phone: (703) 555-4092
   Email: kevin.d.park.civ@us.navy.mil

---

SECTION C - STATEMENT OF WORK

Title: Advanced Distributed Acoustic Sensing for Undersea Domain Awareness

The contractor shall design, fabricate, and test a prototype distributed acoustic
sensing (DAS) system using fiber-optic cables for passive detection and classification
of undersea vehicles in littoral environments. Key tasks include: (1) development of
a novel interrogator unit achieving 10 dB improvement in signal-to-noise ratio over
current DAS systems, (2) machine learning algorithms for real-time target classification,
and (3) at-sea testing at the Naval Undersea Warfare Center (NUWC) range in Newport, RI.

---

SECTION D - PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE

Base Period: 15 January 2025 through 14 January 2027 (24 months)
Option Period 1: 15 January 2027 through 14 January 2028 (12 months)
Period of Performance Start: 15 January 2025
Period of Performance End: 14 January 2027

---

4.  Subcontracts                          $185,000
    a. Naval Postgraduate School           $125,000
       (ML algorithm validation)
    b. Fiber Optics Inc.                    $60,000
       (specialty cable fabrication)

5.  Materials and Supplies                 $64,800
    a. Fiber optic components               $28,000
    b. Electronic components                $22,800
    c. Lab consumables                      $14,000

6.  Equipment                             $82,500
    a. DAS interrogator prototype parts     $52,500
    b. Hydrophone reference array           $30,000

7.  Travel                                $38,400
    a. NUWC Newport test events (4 trips)   $24,000
    b. ONR program reviews                   $8,400
    c. ASA conference                        $6,000

8.  Other Direct Costs                     $34,500
    a. NUWC range time rental               $18,000
    b. Secure computing services             $9,500
    c. Technical report preparation          $7,000

9.  TOTAL DIRECT COSTS                   $2,043,450

10. G&A (on subcontracts only, 5.2%)      $9,620
    G&A Rate: 5.2%

11. TOTAL COST                           $2,053,070

12. Fixed Fee (6.55%)                     $134,380
    Fee/Profit: $134,380

13. TOTAL ESTIMATED COST PLUS FEE:       $2,187,450

expected_values.Statement of Work Summary: The contractor shall design, fabricate, and test a prototype distributed acoustic sensing (DAS) system using fiber-optic cables for passive detection and classification of undersea vehicles in littoral environments.

test_cases.label: DARPA Research Agreement - AI Cybersecurity (CyberForge Labs)
test_cases.source_text:

    0001       Applied Research: Autonomous     1          Lot   $1,875,340   $1,875,340
               Adversarial Threat Detection
               and Response (AATR)

    TOTAL ESTIMATED COST PLUS FEE: $1,875,340

16. TOTAL AMOUNT OF CONTRACT: $1,875,340

Security Classification: Unclassified
Distribution Statement A: Approved for public release; distribution unlimited.
DFARS 252.227-7013 and 252.227-7014 apply (Technical Data and Computer Software rights).

---

SECTION B - SUPPLIES OR SERVICES AND PRICES/COSTS

Contract Type: Cost-Plus-Fixed-Fee (CPFF)
Award Number: HR0011-25-C-0089

Principal Investigator: Dr. Anika Patel, Ph.D.
   Founder and Chief Technology Officer
   CyberForge Labs, LLC
   Phone: (571) 555-6723
   Email: apatel@cyberforgelabs.com

Government Program Manager: Lt. Col. Marcus J. Rivera
   DARPA Information Innovation Office (I2O)
   Phone: (571) 555-1847
   Email: marcus.rivera@darpa.mil

---

SECTION C - STATEMENT OF WORK

Title: Autonomous Adversarial Threat Detection and Response (AATR)

The contractor shall develop and demonstrate an AI-driven cybersecurity system capable
of autonomously detecting, classifying, and responding to adversarial threats in real
time across heterogeneous military networks. The system shall leverage large language
models fine-tuned on network traffic data to identify zero-day exploits with a false
positive rate below 0.1%. Research tasks include: (1) development of a domain-specific
foundation model for network security, (2) adversarial red-team evaluation against
state-of-the-art attack frameworks, and (3) integration demonstration on a DoD
representative network testbed.

---

SECTION D - PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE

Phase 1 (Base): 01 March 2025 through 28 February 2027 (24 months)
Period of Performance Start: 01 March 2025
Period of Performance End: 28 February 2027

---

SECTION J - COST/PRICE SUMMARY

COST ELEMENT SUMMARY (Base Period - Phase 1)

1.  Direct Labor                          $624,000
    a. Principal Investigator (50%)        $175,000
    b. ML Research Scientists (2)          $280,000
    c. Security Engineers (2)              $169,000

2.  Fringe Benefits (32%)                 $199,680

3.  Overhead (45% of Labor + Fringe)      $370,656

4.  Subcontracts                          $120,000
    a. University of Maryland CSCR         $120,000
       (red team evaluation services)

5.  Materials and Supplies                 $18,500
    a. Network testing equipment            $10,500
    b. Software licenses                     $8,000

6.  Equipment                             $95,000
    a. GPU compute cluster (8x A100)        $78,000
    b. Network testbed hardware             $17,000

7.  Travel                                $24,400
    a. DARPA program reviews (quarterly)    $14,400
    b. Test site visits (Fort Meade)         $6,000
    c. Conference (IEEE S&P)                 $4,000

8.  Other Direct Costs                     $42,800
    a. Cloud computing (AWS GovCloud)       $32,000
    b. Secure facility costs                 $6,800
    c. Technical publications                $4,000

9.  TOTAL DIRECT COSTS                   $1,495,036

10. G&A (12.8% of total costs less subs)  $175,924
    G&A Rate: 12.8%
    Base: (Total direct - subcontracts) = $1,375,036

11. TOTAL COST                           $1,670,960

12. Fixed Fee (12.23%)                    $204,380
    Fee/Profit: $204,380

13. TOTAL ESTIMATED COST PLUS FEE:       $1,875,340

expected_values.Statement of Work Summary: The contractor shall develop and demonstrate an AI-driven cybersecurity system capable of autonomously detecting, classifying, and responding to adversarial threats in real time across heterogeneous military networks.
